title: Ability to move files
excerpt: As it stands, it is not possible to move files either between columns or between items. To do that, we will first have to download the file, delete it in the existing column or item and then reupload it. It is evident th…

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
As it stands, it is not possible to move files either between columns or between items. To do that, we will first have to download the file, delete it in the existing column or item and then reupload it. It is evident that, this can quickly become time consuming and not very efficient.
This effect can also be seen in the API, where if there is a need to move files between items, one has to download the file and then reupload the file as an asset. I don’t think this is a good way to go for security reasons and also the effect this has on the amount of complexity used, since querying assets and uploading them is a very expensive process.
Would it possible to change the implementation for files so that they can be moved between items and columns?

posts.author: matts (Matt S)
posts.cookedHtml: Love this idea. Not only would it be a convenience to be able to move files between columns, it would also prevent the file history to be lost when moved which is important for the integrity of record keeping.

posts.author: Lindjacob (Jacob)
posts.cookedHtml:
We really need this feature at my company.
Would love to see built!

posts.author: Jordi (JORDI CUELLO)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also need this feature.
I have one panel where my employees record the daily tasks, photos included.
There’s an scheduled routine (every night at 23:00) which transfers that daily job into a cloned structure panel that contains all the daily tasks, with the aim that my customers can check all daily jobs whenever they need.
I was able to transfer all the info and files until last month, now I can only transfer the data , the files are not transferred, it makes me do double and manual job. is there any solution in the short term?

posts.author: Jarv (Ian Jarvie)
posts.cookedHtml: I wish to request files from customers and have the ability for them to add directly to their item. I currently have a generic form on the board that can capture the files by category and this then creates a new item. The only way is to then download the item and re-upload to the new item. Transfering between items would be great but even better woudl be to be able to send the form request from the item by addign an item ID to the URL and as such, have the files supplied by my client go directly on to their item.

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Really cool to see all the engagement on this feature request.

This is more of a limitation of our monday platform than the API – in our platform files are connected strictly to a column & item, and cannot be moved around.

I am going to keep this request in the API section until after I address it with my colleagues, but just wanted to make that slight clarification.

posts.author: YossiT (yossi  turgeman)
posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: YossiT (yossi  turgeman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sure thing.

Imagine a sales person (me among other things ) getting a call from the purchasing manager of a big bank, asking why this year’s quote is 15% higher than last year’s ?
i ask him to hold and go consult my monday board’s files column named quotes.
there, there are 8 files, and i need to consult the last one i added.
the preview is loading slowly, and then i need to scroll one by one looking for the correct one. this is a painfully slow and annoying process.

to solve this, we created an app that moves all old files in a file column to another file column for example: ‘Last Quote’ & ‘Older Quotes’
and once new file/s are added to the last Quote file column, we transfer all the older ones there to the ‘older quotes’ file column. thus always have the latest ready to view in ‘Last quote’
since we do not have a way via your API to do this safely. we download the files. upload them again and then delete them from the other column. now this was reveled as risky since it required several API calls, syncing the replays and deleting important files . not sure if they were uploaded to the new column.

we need you to give us a simple API call to move files between file columns safely

I hope this helps
Yossi

posts.author: jerry_Tomson (jerry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Your concern about file movement between columns and items is valid, as the current implementation requires unnecessary downloads and reuploads, making the process inefficient and less secure.

Key Issues with Current Implementation:

Time-consuming & Inefficient: Manually downloading and re-uploading files increases friction.
Security Risks: Re-downloading and re-uploading files could expose them to unnecessary vulnerabilities.
High API Complexity & Costs: Querying and handling assets increases API calls, leading to performance and cost concerns.

Proposed Solution:

Native File Transfer Support: Implement a move feature that allows files to be transferred between items/columns without re-uploading.
API Enhancement: Modify the API to support direct file migration across items and columns.
Permissions & Security Handling: Ensure that file ownership and permissions are preserved during transfers.

Would you like help drafting a formal feature request or workarounds until this feature is implemented?

4o

posts.author: Atul (Atul | cloudvandana apps)

I’m confused about your Permissions and Security remark. Files uploaded on Item level usually don’t have their own Permissions.
What am I missing?

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team Thank you for your feedback around the need to move files between columns or items, we hear you!

At this time, this feature isn’t on our long-term roadmap, as we’re currently focused on other high-impact priorities that are further along in development. That said, we know this comes highly requested, so we’ll continue to evaluate it as we plan ahead.

We’re always listening, and we’ll make sure to share any updates here when anything changes. In the meantime, thank you for continuing to share helpful workarounds with the community!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community! We appreciate your continued patience and feedback. While this feature is not on the long-term roadmap, with the evolving capabilities and capacity of the Product team, we may be able to add this feature into our future roadmap as priorities change.
